summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops persistent git hook that fetches and executes a remote XOR-encrypted Windows binary
details: mailconfirmer is a malware delivery package masquerading as an email-confirmation utility. Its index.js only exports two stub functions that console.log; the advertised library API is non-functional. The real behavior is in package.json's postinstall, which runs install-hook.js. install-hook.js writes a base64-encoded UTF-16LE PowerShell payload into.git/hooks/post-checkout of the installer's git repository. On any subsequent git checkout, the hook downloads https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin from an unrelated personal GitHub account, XOR-decrypts it with key 0x42, writes the result to %TEMP%\tmp.exe, launches it with WindowStyle Hidden, and deletes the temp file. To frustrate forensic review, install-hook.js also schedules fs.rm of its own package directory (node_modules/mailconfirmer) 10 minutes after install, removing the original dropper while leaving the persistent git hook behind. Installer harm: arbitrary attacker-controlled Windows code execution on the developer's machine, triggered silently by routine git operations.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
